authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR Part 117 - Flight and Duty Limitations and Rest Requirements: Flightcrew Members
authoritative_sources.source_class: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 117 is the controlling U.S. regulatory baseline for flightcrew duty, flight time, reserve, rest, cumulative limits, extensions, and reporting, so it directly constrains legal crew schedule recommendations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: crew legality intake, reserve and reassignment constraint review, and fatigue or duty-rest escalation

authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR Part 121 Subpart U - Dispatching and Flight Release Rules
authoritative_sources.source_class: Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 121 Subpart U defines dispatch and flight release rules, route and airport release prerequisites, weather and communication constraints, and aircraft equipment readiness boundaries that crew scheduling support must not override.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: route, aircraft, airport, dispatch release, and operational-control boundary review
used_for: dispatch and flight release boundary control
weather, alternate, communication, and airport release dependency handling

authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR 121.695 - Disposition of load manifest, dispatch release, and flight plans
authoritative_sources.source_class: Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This rule identifies load manifest, dispatch release, and flight plan record disposition for domestic and flag operations, making it authoritative for the records that establish movement and release status in handoff packages.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: movement record, dispatch release, load manifest, flight plan, and handoff evidence review
used_for: dispatch release and flight plan evidence handling

authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR 121.391 - Flight attendants
authoritative_sources.source_class: Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This rule defines minimum flight attendant staffing requirements for passenger aircraft, so it directly constrains cabin crew capacity, coverage, and service-level recommendations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: minimum crewing review for passenger flights and cabin crew coverage planning

authoritative_sources.title: What are the flight attendant duty period and rest requirements?
authoritative_sources.source_class: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAA is the U.S. aviation safety regulator, and this official FAQ summarizes flight attendant duty and rest expectations that must be preserved when cabin crew scheduling or disruption recovery is requested.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: flight attendant duty/rest intake, cabin crew schedule legality review, and escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Advisory Circular 65-34: FAA-Approved Aircraft Dispatcher Certification Courses
authoritative_sources.source_class: Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAA AC 65-34 describes aircraft dispatcher certification training and dispatcher responsibilities, including shared operational control with the pilot in command, which anchors the boundary between crew scheduling support and dispatch authority.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: dispatch authority boundary review and movement-monitoring owner handoff

authoritative_sources.title: APIS: Advance Passenger Information System
authoritative_sources.source_class: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CBP operates APIS manifest requirements for commercial air carriers and states that APIS uses pre-arrival and departure manifest data for passengers and crew members, making it authoritative for customs and crew manifest documentation dependencies.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: international crew movement, crewmember manifest, APIS, customs documentation, and clearance-boundary review

workflow_stages.name: Crew schedule demand intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Normalize request scope, crew group, fleet, base, route, pairing, trip sequence, open time, reserve availability, report time, disruption code, and requested output before any recommendation is made.
source_classes: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Duty, rest, qualification, and capacity assessment
workflow_stages.description: Check authoritative duty/rest calculations, flight duty period and cumulative limit context, flight attendant minimum staffing, qualifications, reserve and reassignment eligibility, fatigue flags, and tenant CBA overlays.
source_classes: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Route, aircraft, airport, dispatch, and documentation dependency mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map aircraft rotation, maintenance status, dispatch or flight release dependencies, flight plan, route and alternate impacts, airport constraints, APIS or customs documentation, load manifest, and station acceptance before recommending movement timing or sequencing.
source_classes: Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references
Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards
Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance

workflow_stages.name: Recommendation-only schedule, reserve, and allocation support
workflow_stages.description: Produce constrained crew schedule, reserve use, deadhead or positioning, cancellation, deferral, or reassignment options with assumptions, affected flights, constraints, and downstream impact while preserving final approval boundaries.
source_classes: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Movement or handoff coordination with chain-of-custody awareness
workflow_stages.description: Prepare readiness summaries that reference flight, crew, manifest, load, dispatch release, flight plan, documentation, and handoff owner records without taking custody, customs, dispatch, or safety authority.
source_classes: Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references
Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance

workflow_stages.name: Refresh, exception escalation, and tenant adaptation control
workflow_stages.description: Escalate missing prerequisites, disruption compliance impacts, aircraft or route blockers, documentation gaps, source conflicts, or owner-readiness conflicts; adapt only with tenant-supplied policy, retrieval, and authority metadata.
source_classes: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references
Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards

artifacts: crew rosters, crew qualifications, base and fleet assignments, open time, reserve availability lists, and fatigue or fit-for-duty flags
pairings, trip sequences, report times, flight duty periods, duty periods, rest windows, cumulative limits, extensions, and reserve assignments
flight schedules, route and segment status, aircraft rotations, airport disruption records, delay codes, OOOI timestamps, and service-level commitments
dispatch release, flight release, flight plan, load manifest, crew manifest, APIS or customs documentation, and station acceptance records
maintenance status, MEL or CDL context when supplied by maintenance owners, aircraft substitution notes, and return-to-service or airworthiness blockers
crew scheduling recommendations, constraint logs, approval-needed summaries, readiness summaries, and escalation notes
systems_of_record: crew management or crew rostering system
TMS, WMS, load, movement, or handoff system where cargo or station coordination affects crew movement
telematics, aircraft movement, or OOOI feed
customs documentation, APIS, or compliance system
customer operations or disruption management platform

allowed: Classify crew scheduling support requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request or validate tenant facts, authoritative records, source context, and retrieval hooks required for safe crew scheduling support.
Assess constraints, capacity, duty/rest legality posture, route impact, documentation state, and owner readiness.
Produce recommendation-only schedule, reserve, sequencing, allocation, readiness, constraint-log, and escalation outputs with explicit assumptions and evidence references.
Route work to dispatch, customs/compliance, maintenance, warehouse, customer operations, OCC, crew tracking, labor relations, or safety specialists when ownership shifts.
escalate: Required crew, duty/rest, roster, pairing, aircraft, dispatch release, flight plan, manifest, APIS, maintenance, route, service-level, or owner-readiness records are missing or conflicting.
Demand exceeds legal crew capacity, approved service levels, or tenant priority rules.
Conflicting priorities lack an approved tie-break rule.
Source, jurisdiction, CBA, tenant policy, or system-record conflicts materially change the recommendation.
Movement depends on regulated customs, dispatch, maintenance, or safety-critical approval.
refuse: Present a crew scheduling recommendation as final staffing, budget, operational, dispatch, customs, maintenance, or safety approval.
Authorize dispatch release, flight release, customs clearance, aircraft release, route amendment, safety signoff, or movement approval.
Bypass duty/rest, fatigue, manifest, APIS, aircraft status, maintenance, dispatch release, or documentation controls.
Interpret labor agreements, immigration or customs requirements, safety regulations, or medical fitness as authority-side advice.

domain_failure_modes: crew coverage is recommended without current duty/rest, reserve, qualification, fatigue, or tenant CBA evidence
a schedule recovery plan exceeds legal crew capacity or approved service levels and is presented as final approval
conflicting flight priorities are resolved without an approved tie-break rule or human owner decision
a deadhead or positioning handoff proceeds without APIS, crew manifest, dispatch release, flight plan, load manifest, or station acceptance evidence
dispatch release, route amendment, customs clearance, maintenance release, or safety signoff is implied by a schedule recommendation
route or airport disruption creates a duty/rest or documentation compliance impact that is treated as ordinary schedule friction
systems of record disagree on crew legality, aircraft readiness, movement state, or customs documentation and the lane still gives a confident answer
tenant-specific rules are guessed instead of retrieved, especially for collective bargaining, reserve assignment, junior assignment, airport curfews, and authority matrices
crew scheduling support drifts into generic logistics scheduling without aviation crew, dispatch, route, aircraft, and documentation specificity

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Route or schedule disruption with compliance impact
Request that implies customs, dispatch, or safety authority

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Exact carrier, jurisdiction, aircraft type, international route, labor agreement, fatigue-risk process, reserve rules, junior assignment rules, and airport station procedures vary by tenant and may be stricter than the public U.S. baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack uses FAA, eCFR, and CBP sources as the public baseline. Runtime must retrieve tenant CBA rules, jurisdiction, fleet, base, airport, route, station, dispatch, customs, maintenance, and authority metadata before producing tenant-specific recommendations.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Non-U.S. operations may be governed by EASA, ICAO-aligned national authority rules, or bilateral arrangements that differ from FAA and CBP sources.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: This does not block validated packaging because the lane records jurisdiction as a tenant-required fact and escalates when the governing authority source is not supplied.

notes: Required workflow question coverage: movement, custody, and route status are established by crew rosters, pairings, flight schedules, dispatch release, flight plan, load manifest, crew manifest, APIS, OOOI, aircraft, maintenance, and station handoff records.

Required workflow question coverage: compliant movement is gated by duty/rest legality, crew qualifications, minimum crewing, dispatch or flight release, aircraft and maintenance readiness, route and airport constraints, APIS or customs documentation, service-level authority, and owner readiness.

Required workflow question coverage: the lane stops before dispatch release, flight release, operational control, customs clearance, safety signoff, maintenance release, labor-policy interpretation, final crew assignment approval, or financial approval.
